objective: Isolate the dataflow cost around the accepted C++ EGLImage-wrapper Regular05 path without changing the accepted visual output semantics.

accepted_dependency.quality_reference: VPI Python allocated-image + explicit BGR8 correctness path
accepted_dependency.matrix_role: performance/dataflow diagnostic matrix only; not the strongest stabilization-quality baseline
accepted_dependency.quality_anchor: resid_r15_s07 is the accepted stabilization-strength baseline for Regular05 and must be used for quality comparison
accepted_dependency.claim_boundary: Regular gate C++ path is frozen for inclusion replay only when studying dataflow cost. Do not use inclusion replay to judge current stabilization strength.

frozen_variables: matrix file for this dataflow contract: inclusion_source_to_dest.csv
quality baseline for stabilization-strength checks: resid_r15_s07, not inclusion_source_to_dest.csv
do not change BGR8 correctness reference
do not run optical-flow acceleration inside this contract

baseline_observation.note: The measured bottleneck is the dataflow stage around VPI, not the PerspectiveWarp kernel alone.

transform_only_probe.steady_state_total_ms: about 2.5 to 2.8 ms for three NvBufSurfTransform calls
transform_only_probe.interpretation: Transform cost is significant but does not explain the full 10.5 ms EGLImage stage; wrapper/map/submit/sync overhead remains the next target.

egl_map_probe.interpretation: EGLImage map/unmap cost is small and does not explain the accepted EGLImage stage cost; wrapper creation and VPI submit/sync remain the next target.

vpi_wrapper_create_probe.interpretation: VPI wrapper lifecycle is a major component of the accepted EGLImage stage cost; wrapper reuse removed real cost but was unsafe in this EGLImage/NvBufSurface path.

identity_warp_probe.interpretation: Identity warp is only slightly faster than inclusion warp; matrix complexity is not the main dataflow bottleneck.

surface_format_probe.interpretation: The accepted transform sandwich is required by observed main/scratch format, layout, and pitch mismatch. Direct NvBuffer wrapping must use format-matched input/output pairs.

blocklinear_pair_probe.limited_range_error: Only image formats with full range are accepted
blocklinear_pair_probe.full_range_error: Unsupported image format for BT601_ER BLOCK16_LINEAR
blocklinear_pair_probe.interpretation: VPI PerspectiveWarp does not accept the tested block-linear scratch pairs; pitch-linear NV12_ER scratch remains required for VPI compatibility.

pitch_encoder_probe.interpretation: NVENC command returns success but pitch-linear main-chain output is visually invalid; block-linear main chain remains required.

allowed_experiments: measure repeated wrapper creation versus reusable wrapper behavior
measure NvBufSurfTransform input and output costs separately
test format-matched NvBuffer input/output pairs only if the format match is explicit
add logging instrumentation to a copied MMAPI sample directory
forbidden_claims: performance improvement without same-input timing and quality sanity
success_criteria: black_border_p95 remains below 0.01 or any gray-metric flag is visually classified
timing table separates handoff, VPI warp, transform, wrapper, and wall time
if an experiment fails, the exact VPI/MMAPI error is recorded as negative evidence
stop_reasons: format mismatch blocks the attempted path
quality regression relative to accepted EGLImage path
would require broad MMAPI rewrite beyond a copied sample
